other_observers: Heather Morris, Doug Shoffner; Dave Leatherman
duration: Visual-15 seconds Audial-sang 5 times over 45 minutes
specific_location: Very east end of the Palisade River Trail in a grove of cottonwoods next to the Colorado River. There was a dirt boat ramp just west of the grove.
description: The short view I had of this bird was downhill in thick undergrowth. I was looking down on the back of the bird. I saw a uniform olive-brownish back, a slightly lighter brown head, and three jet black stripes running from the forehead area to the back of the head. The bill was rather long and needle-like.Behaviors: The bird was constantly on the move between the leaf-covered ground and low branches. Difficult to observe much behavior as the bird was in thick undergrowth and in sight only for brief moments.Call: A rapid, metallic buzz almost reminding me of a cicada.Plumage: Adult
